steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The closing sentence begins with "Torchy Brown thus shows how" — the word thus signals a conclusion. The passage has been demonstrating how Ormes presented Black American experiences through her comic work, and this sentence wraps that up.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
Check each option against what the passage actually does:
  • Choice A — To show how Ormes's Torchy Brown inspired other Black women to write comic strips in the 1930s: The passage never mentions other Black women comic strip writers — this is unsupported. ✗
  • Choice B — To illustrate how the subjects Ormes addressed in her comic strips changed over the course of her career: The passage says Ormes continued to address the same kinds of issues throughout her career, not that the subjects changed. ✗
  • Choice C — To give an example of how Ormes presented the experiences of Black Americans in her comic strips: The passage gives Torchy Brown as a concrete example of Ormes using comics to comment on issues affecting Black Americans — this matches the main purpose. ✓
  • Choice D — To claim that several characters in Torchy Brown were based on people that Ormes knew personally: The passage makes no claim about the inspiration for any characters. ✗
That matches Choice C — To give an example of how Ormes presented the experiences of Black Americans in her comic strips.
